english: 1. a large feather, esp. quill-feather, used for an ornament of arrows, as a charm etc.; sgro-mdóṅs (sgro mdongs) peacock's feather, as a badge of dignity. 2. to elevate, exalt, increase; Cs.: to exaggerate. Was. however has p. (305): 'Vorurtheil (Anerkennung des Nichtwahren), Gegensatz: skur-°débs (skur 'debs) Lästerung (Leugnung des Wahren)', and p. (297): 'sgro-skúr (sgro skur) Verneinen und Lästerung'. Cs. renders sgro-skúr (sgro skur) by 'exaggerated praise and blame'; sgro-skúr °débs-ba (sgro skur 'debs ba) occurs also in Mil. The phrase sgro-°dógs γcód-pa (sgro 'dogs gcod pa) might therefore be rendered: to put an end to overrating and to prejudice; this meaning, however, does not suit in every instance, and acc. to expressions heard from people in C., sgro-°dógs γcód-pa (sgro 'dogs gcod pa) would signify: to turn to account, to work one's way up, to contest for a prize. Finally ought to be mentioned that acc. to Schr. sgro-°dógs spyód-pa (sgro 'dogs spyod pa) (sic) denotes 'logic'. A connection between these heterogeneous significations is not discernible, but a clew may perhaps be found hereafter. - 3. sack, bag (?), tál-sgro (thal sgro) Glr. was explained by: a sack full of ashes.
C. the little bubbles in sparkling beverages, *čáṅ-la dó-ga duq* (*chang la do ga dug*) the beer sparkles.

I. sbst. 1. Wdṅ., acc. to Sch. the bark of a species of willow, but prob. the same as gró-ga (gro ga). - 2. C. the penis. II. vb., pf. bsgros (bsgros), fut. bsgro (bsgro), imp. sgro (sgro) Lexx. w.e., Cs.: to debate, discuss, so that it would be only another form of bgro-ba (bgro ba); but in C. *do-śé' jhé'-pa* (*do she' jhe' pa*) is said to mean: to talk at random, to chatter away thoughtlessly.

cord, rope, for tying, fettering; fetters Mil. and C.; lčags-sgróg (lcags sgrog) iron fetters, chain; lčags-sgróg lág-pa sbrél-nas (lcags sgrog lag pa sbrel nas) the hands tied or chained together; lčags-sgróg-pa (lcags sgrog pa) a convict put in irons; šiṅ-sgróg (shing sgrog) fetters made of twisted twigs Cs.; lham-sgróg (lham sgrog) shoe-strap, lace, latchet. - sgróg-gu (sgrog gu), sgró-gu (sgro gu), W. *róg-bu* (*rog bu*), string, strap, for binding, fastening, strapping; Sch. also button; sgrog-ríl (sgrog ril) Sch. button, sgrog-ríl sgróg-pa (sgrog ril sgrog pa) to button up.

pf. bsgrags (bsgrags), fut. bsgrag (bsgrag), imp. sgrags(s) (sgrags(s)), to call, call out, call to Dzl. and elsewh.; to publish, proclaim, promulgate, γtam-snyán (gtam snyan) good news Mil.; ši-bai γtam bsgrágs-na (shi ba'i gtam bsgrags na) if his death becomes known, Tar.; čos sgróg-pa (chos sgrog pa), resp. čós-kyi sgrog-gléṅ mdzád-pa (chos kyi sgrog gleng mdzad pa) Glr. to preach; dril-sgrog-pa (dril sgrog pa) to publish by ringing a bell, to publish, proclaim; sgróg-pa-po (sgrog pa po) a proclaimer, a preacher Cs. - 2. to shout, to scream, ṅú-skad drág-por sgrog (ngu skad drag por sgrog) (the infant) weeps and screams Lt. - 3. C. (in W. only resp.) to read, γsuṅ sgróg-pa (gsung sgrog pa) to read words of Buddha Ma.; even: séms-kyis sgróg-pa (sems kyis sgrog pa) to read silently. - 4. to bind, like sgrógs-pa (sgrogs pa); v. also sgrog (sgrog) extr.
another form of gród-pa (grod pa) to go; not much used.

a lamp, lantern, torch, sgron-mé (sgron me) a burning lamp, (prop. a lamp-fire); often fig. - sgrón-pa (sgron pa) vb. to light, to kindle, dpé-ča-la me sgrón-nas (dpe cha la me sgron nas) lighting (burning) the book Pth. - sgron-bskál (sgron bskal) the enlightened age Cs., opp. to mun-bskál (mun bskal) the dark age. - sgron-drégs (sgron dregs) lamp-black. - sgron-(me-)šiṅ (sgron (me-)shing) Sch. the yew-leaved fir, Pinus picea, which tree, however, is scarcely known in Tibet; in Sik. it denotes Pinus longifolia, and prob. also in every other province, the most resinous species of coniferous trees prevailing there.
pf. and fut. bsgron (bsgron) 1. to cover, to lay over, adorn, decorate Glr. - 2. to light, to kindle, v. sgrón-ma (sgron ma).

box, chest, trunk, coffer = sgam (sgam); sgróm-bu (sgrom bu) a small box or chest: smyug-sgróm (smyug sgrom) Cs. = γzébs-ma (gzebs ma) a chest or trunk made of bamboo; ro-sgróm (ro sgrom), rús-pai sgrom (rus pa'i sgrom) Zam. coffin.
pf. and fut. bsgral (bsgral) 1. to rescue, deliver, save, las (las) from, out of, sgról-bai ded-dpón-du γyur (sgrol ba'i ded dpon du gyur) he becomes a guide to salvation Glr. - 2. to lead,
